ransom_note.content:
===============================================================
                    YOUR NETWORK HAS BEEN LOCKED
===============================================================

Pinnacle Manufacturing Corp
Victim ID: PMC-8A3F-29D1-CC47

All of your files have been encrypted with military-grade encryption.
Do NOT attempt to decrypt them yourself — you will permanently damage
your data.

We have also downloaded 847 GB of sensitive data from your network,
including:
  - Employee personal records and payroll data
  - Engineering blueprints and trade secrets
  - Customer contracts and financial statements
  - Internal emails and executive communications

If you do not cooperate, this data will be published on our leak site
for anyone to download.

TO RESTORE YOUR FILES:

1. Download the Tor Browser from https://www.torproject.org
2. Navigate to: http://fakeonion1234abc5678.onion/chat
3. Enter your Victim ID: PMC-8A3F-29D1-CC47
4. Follow the instructions to arrange payment

PRICE: The cost to decrypt your entire network will be discussed
in the chat. The sooner you contact us, the lower the price.

DEADLINE: You have 72 hours from the time of this message to make
initial contact. After that:
  - Day 3-7: Price doubles
  - Day 7+: Data publication begins
  - Day 14: Decryption keys are permanently deleted

DO NOT:
  - Contact law enforcement (we will know and publish immediately)
  - Hire a recovery company (they will just contact us and charge
    you a fee on top of our price)
  - Shut down your systems (this may damage encrypted files)
  - Delete this file (you need the Victim ID)

We are a professional organization. We have successfully helped
hundreds of companies restore their operations. Pay promptly and
you will receive a fully functional decryptor within 24 hours.

===============================================================
                        DO NOT IGNORE THIS
===============================================================

network_iocs.note: C2 callback over HTTPS — first seen at 01:30 UTC

lolbas: rclone.exe (renamed to svchost.exe) — data exfiltration to Mega.nz
wmic.exe — remote process execution across domain
vssadmin.exe — shadow copy deletion on all servers
certutil.exe — download of additional tools from C2
powershell.exe — encoded command execution for reconnaissance
